ReleaseLabel: Set 047 — state-file schema v4 (extension TBD + dabbler-ai-router TBD)
Notes: Ad-hoc UAT for the Set 047 Session 5 user-visible surface: the new spec.md `prerequisites:` field and the Session Set Explorer's [BLOCKED BY PREREQS] badge derived from cross-referencing each set's prereqs against its dependants' status. Also exercises the v4 on-disk schema emitted by TS writers (synthesizeNotStartedState, ensureSessionStateFile, cancelSessionSet, restoreSessionSet) — the operator inspects an emitted file and confirms the v4 shape lands on disk. Schema notes: each item carries `Passes` (canonical truth used by the UAT Checklist Editor app) and `Result` (set to 'pending' for the Dabbler extension's pending-count parser). Update both fields together when marking an item complete.

Review.ItemLabel: [BLOCKED BY PREREQS] renders on dependant when prereq is in-progress
Review.HumanAction: Author a fresh session set 047-uat-blocked under docs/session-sets/ with a spec.md whose Session Set Configuration block declares `prerequisites:` with `- slug: <any-in-progress-set>` and `condition: complete`. Run `Dabbler: Refresh Session Sets View` from the Command Palette and inspect the 047-uat-blocked row.
Review.Expectation: The row description includes the literal text `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]` (alongside touched-date / UAT / force-closed / needs-migration badges as applicable). The prereq's own row does NOT carry the badge.

Review.ItemLabel: Badge clears when prereq target flips to complete
Review.HumanAction: From the previous fixture, flip the prereq target's session-state.json `status` to `complete` (either via close_session or by hand-editing). Re-run `Dabbler: Refresh Session Sets View`.
Review.Expectation: The 047-uat-blocked row no longer renders `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]`. Other badges (UAT, etc.) persist if applicable.

Review.ItemLabel: Unknown prereq slug keeps the row blocked
Review.HumanAction: Author a session set whose `prerequisites:` declares `- slug: 999-does-not-exist` and `condition: complete`. Refresh the view.
Review.Expectation: The row carries `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]` (the typo / missing-set case stays blocked rather than silently unblocking — a typo would otherwise mask the very dependency error the field is for).

Review.HumanAction: Take the 047-uat-blocked fixture from the first item; flip ITS own session-state.json `status` to `complete` (sessions[] all complete) while leaving the prereq target in-progress. Refresh.
Review.Expectation: 047-uat-blocked is now in the Complete bucket and renders no `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]` badge (the dependency is no longer actionable on a closed set).

Review.ItemLabel: Spec without prerequisites parses to empty/null
Review.HumanAction: Author a session set without any `prerequisites:` key in its Session Set Configuration block (the common case). Refresh.
Review.Expectation: The row renders normally with no `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]` badge. Internally `set.prerequisites` is null (verifiable via the read-side unit test).

Review.HumanAction: Author a session set with two prereq entries (`- slug: A` complete + `- slug: B` complete), where A is complete and B is in-progress. Refresh.
Review.Expectation: The dependant row renders `[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]` because B is not yet satisfied. Completing B would clear the badge.

Review.HumanAction: Create a fresh session set folder with only a spec.md (no session-state.json) and totalSessions=2 declared. Open the workspace in the extension so the Explorer triggers lazy-synth.
Review.Expectation: The session-state.json that lands on disk has `"schemaVersion": 4`, top-level `status: "not-started"`, NO top-level `lifecycleState` / `currentSession` / `totalSessions` / `completedSessions` / `startedAt` / `completedAt` / `orchestrator` / `verificationVerdict` keys, and a `sessions[]` array of 2 entries each with `startedAt: null` / `completedAt: null` / `orchestrator: null` / `verificationVerdict: null`.

Review.ItemLabel: Lazy-synth backfill from change-log writes v4 complete
Review.HumanAction: Create a session-set folder with spec.md (totalSessions=2) + change-log.md but NO session-state.json. Refresh the Explorer.
Review.Expectation: The lazy-synthesized state file is v4 (schemaVersion: 4), top-level status: complete, sessions[] has 2 entries all with status: complete. NO top-level lifecycleState / completedAt / completedSessions / currentSession.

Review.ItemLabel: Lazy-synth backfill from activity-log writes v4 in-progress
Review.HumanAction: Create a session-set folder with spec.md (totalSessions=3) + activity-log.json (with one entry, `dateTime: 2026-05-26T08:00:00-04:00`). No session-state.json. Refresh.
Review.Expectation: The state file is v4, top-level status: in-progress, sessions[0].status: in-progress, sessions[0].startedAt: 2026-05-26T08:00:00-04:00 (per-session, NOT top-level), sessions[1].status / sessions[2].status: not-started.

Review.ItemLabel: Right-click Cancel emits v4 with preCancelStatus
Review.HumanAction: Right-click an in-progress session set row → Cancel set. Confirm the modal. Inspect the resulting session-state.json.
Review.Expectation: schemaVersion: 4, status: cancelled, preCancelStatus: in-progress. Per-session orchestrator preserved as historical record (the shim promoted the legacy top-level orchestrator onto the in-progress session before the v4 trim). No top-level lifecycleState / currentSession / orchestrator / startedAt.

Review.ItemLabel: Right-click Restore returns to v4 with pre-cancel status
Review.HumanAction: From the canceled fixture above, right-click → Restore set. Inspect the state file.
Review.Expectation: schemaVersion: 4, status: in-progress (restored from preCancelStatus). preCancelStatus key absent. The v4 sessions[] ledger is intact.

Review.ItemLabel: Legacy v3 sets render correctly alongside v4 sets
Review.HumanAction: In a workspace with a mix of v3 session-state.json files (e.g., older session sets that haven't been migrated yet) and v4 files written by the new TS writers, open the Explorer.
Review.Expectation: All rows render their state buckets correctly (Cancelled / Complete / In Progress / Not Started). The v3 sets show the same fraction / touched-date / UAT badges they always have. The v3-shim reader is transparent — operators see no schema-version distinction at the row level.

Review.ItemLabel: (needs migration) chip still surfaces on v3 sets
Review.HumanAction: Locate a row whose state file is still canonical v3 (schemaVersion: 3 with sessions[]). Verify the chip / right-click menu.
Review.Expectation: The row carries the `(needs migration)` chip. The right-click menu offers `Migrate to v4 schema`. Selecting it converts the file to canonical v4 and refreshes the row (chip clears).
